i keep coming back to the kitchen. her kitchen. Marisol always said the kitchen is where the house keeps its heart and now the house has no heart, just a kettle nobody fills. three weeks since the funeral in Valparaíso and i still reach for my phone every sunday at five like it will ring. it doesn't ring. mom keeps asking if i'm eating and i lie and say yes and then i stand in front of the open fridge for ten minutes looking at nothing. today i found her handwriting on a recipe card, "double the cinnamon, always double the cinnamon", and i sat down on the floor and cried until my chest hurt. i don't know what to do with all the love that has nowhere to go now. that's the thing nobody tells you. the love doesn't leave when the person does. it just stands there in the kitchen with you, holding a recipe card, waiting for a sunday phone call that isn't coming. i miss her voice. i miss being someone's granddaughter. i'm writing this because if i say it out loud in the apartment the walls feel too big.
